description: body.origin_registry MUST be a bare DNS hostname matching the authority component of ctx_id (RFC-ACDP-0002 §3.1). A registry assigns origin_registry as a plain hostname; a consumer validating a retrieved body against acdp-context-body.schema.json MUST accept this form.

checks: origin_registry matches acdp-common.schema.json#/$defs/hostname (LDH labels, dot-separated, no colons)
origin_registry equals the authority component of ctx_id ('registry.example.com')

rationale: Pins the conformant form. origin_registry is the DNS-hostname encoding of the registry identity; capabilities.registry_did carries the did:web form of the same registry. The two encodings are distinct and MUST NOT be interchanged on the wire. Companion negative case: body-002.
